---
title: "Question 3: Predictive Analytics"
author: "Penny Hunter"
date: "2026-05-13"
output: html_document
---

Note: This file is designed to be run after Question  1 and 2 rmd

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo      = TRUE,
  message   = FALSE,
  warning   = FALSE,
  fig.align = "center"
)

library(tidyverse)
library(scales)
```

## 1.0 Define Outcome Variable

```{r}
# Programs spending >5% of filing budget on rebates are classified as high rebate
rebate_pct <- Budget_filing_2020 %>%
  filter(!is.na(`Filing Budget`) & `Filing Budget` > 0) %>%
  mutate(
    rebate_pct  = `Filing Gross End User Rebate` / `Filing Budget` * 100,
    high_rebate = ifelse(rebate_pct > 5, 1, 0)
  )

# Distribution of rebate percentage
summary(rebate_pct$rebate_pct)

# How many programs exceed 5% threshold
rebate_pct %>% count(high_rebate)
```

## 2.0 Build Model Dataset

```{r}
# PA overlap check — budget has 10 PAs, programs has 16
unique(Budget_filing_2020$PA)
unique(programs$PA)

# Join budget to programs — PA.x retained from budget as base dataset
model_data <- rebate_pct %>%
  left_join(programs, by = c("Program ID" = "PrgID")) %>%
  rename(PA = PA.x) %>%
  select(
    `Program ID`, PA, high_rebate, rebate_pct,
    `Filing Budget`, `Excluded From Budget`,
    ProgramStatus, Sector, StartYear, EndYear
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    program_age   = 2020 - StartYear,
    is_open_ended = ifelse(is.na(EndYear), 1, 0),
    is_active     = ifelse(ProgramStatus == "active", 1, 0)
  )

# Confirm row count and missing values after join
nrow(model_data)
colSums(is.na(model_data))
```

## 3.0 Exploratory Analysis — Candidate Predictors

```{r}
# Rebate rate by PA
model_data %>%
  group_by(PA) %>%
  summarise(
    n               = n(),
    pct_high_rebate = round(mean(high_rebate == 1) * 100, 1)
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(pct_high_rebate))

# Rebate rate by Sector
model_data %>%
  group_by(Sector) %>%
  summarise(
    n               = n(),
    pct_high_rebate = round(mean(high_rebate == 1) * 100, 1)
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(pct_high_rebate))

# Rebate rate by program status
model_data %>%
  group_by(ProgramStatus) %>%
  summarise(
    n               = n(),
    pct_high_rebate = round(mean(high_rebate == 1) * 100, 1)
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(pct_high_rebate))

# Budget size vs high rebate
model_data %>%
  group_by(high_rebate) %>%
  summarise(mean_budget = mean(`Filing Budget`, na.rm = TRUE))

# Program age vs high rebate — dropped as predictor, shown here for transparency
model_data %>%
  group_by(high_rebate) %>%
  summarise(mean_age = mean(program_age, na.rm = TRUE))

# Open ended vs high rebate
model_data %>%
  group_by(is_open_ended) %>%
  summarise(
    n               = n(),
    pct_high_rebate = round(mean(high_rebate == 1) * 100, 1)
  )

# Excluded from budget vs high rebate — dropped as predictor, only one value
model_data %>%
  group_by(`Excluded From Budget`) %>%
  summarise(
    n               = n(),
    pct_high_rebate = round(mean(high_rebate == 1) * 100, 1)
  )
```

## 4.0 Claims-Level Features

```{r}
# Aggregate claims to program level and check association with high rebate
claims_features <- claims_record_2020 %>%
  group_by(`Program ID`) %>%
  summarise(
    total_claims        = n(),
    pct_residential     = round(mean(`Measure Residential Flag`, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 1),
    total_kWh           = sum(`First Year Gross kWh`, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_Therm         = sum(`First Year Gross Therm`, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_technology_groups = n_distinct(`Technology Group`),
    n_building_types    = n_distinct(`Building Type`)
  )

# Join to model data
model_data_full <- model_data %>%
  left_join(claims_features, by = "Program ID")

# Match rate
cat("Programs with claims data:", sum(!is.na(model_data_full$total_claims)), "\n")
cat("Programs without claims data:", sum(is.na(model_data_full$total_claims)), "\n")

# Association with high rebate — total_claims shows strongest signal
model_data_full %>%
  group_by(high_rebate) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_total_claims    = mean(total_claims, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_pct_residential = mean(pct_residential, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_kWh             = mean(total_kWh, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_Therm           = mean(total_Therm, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_n_tech_groups   = mean(n_technology_groups, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
```

## 5.0 Logistic Regression Models

```{r}
# Convert categorical variables to factors
model_data_full <- model_data_full %>%
  mutate(
    high_rebate = as.factor(high_rebate),
    PA          = as.factor(PA),
    Sector      = as.factor(Sector)
  )

# Five individual models — one per predictor
m1 <- glm(high_rebate ~ PA,             data = model_data_full, family = binomial)
m2 <- glm(high_rebate ~ Sector,         data = model_data_full, family = binomial)
m3 <- glm(high_rebate ~ `Filing Budget`, data = model_data_full, family = binomial)
m4 <- glm(high_rebate ~ is_open_ended,  data = model_data_full, family = binomial)
m5 <- glm(high_rebate ~ total_claims,   data = model_data_full, family = binomial)
```

## 6.0 Model Comparison

```{r}
# Compare models using AIC and McFadden R²
# McFadden R² = 1 - (log likelihood of model / log likelihood of null)
null_model <- glm(high_rebate ~ 1, data = model_data_full, family = binomial)

model_comparison <- data.frame(
  Model = c("Total Claims", "Sector", "Filing Budget", "Is Open Ended", "PA"),
  AIC   = round(c(AIC(m5), AIC(m2), AIC(m3), AIC(m4), AIC(m1)), 1),
  McFadden_R2 = round(1 - c(
    logLik(m5), logLik(m2), logLik(m3), logLik(m4), logLik(m1)
  ) / logLik(null_model), 3)
) %>%
  arrange(AIC)

print(model_comparison)
```
